text: Git branching is an essential feature of version control that helps teams work together efficiently. In a typical workflow, developers create feature branches to work on new functions or bug fixes without affecting the main codebase. Each feature branch is an isolated copy where changes can be made safely. Once the work is complete, the developer creates a pull request to propose merging their changes into the main branch. This allows other team members to review the code, discuss improvements, and ensure quality before integration. Finally, merging combines the feature branch’s updates with the main branch, keeping the project’s history clear and organized. This process makes collaboration smoother and prevents conflicts.
